quietHours.notes: During these hours, operating a radio, musical instrument, or other sound-making device is generally unlawful if "plainly audible" at a distance of 100 feet from the source.

decibelLimits.measurementNotes: Figures shown are for single-family residential zoning (60 dBA day / 55 dBA night); multifamily and other noise-sensitive zoning generally allow up to 65 dBA day / 60 dBA night. Any sound at 110 dBA or above is prohibited outright. TODO_VERIFY: whether the applicable text for a given address is the City of Miami Code (Ch. 36) or Miami-Dade County Code (Sec. 21-28) — sourcing for these figures blended references to both, and the City and County codes are not identical documents.

ordinance.name: City of Miami Code of Ordinances — Noise
ordinance.citation: Miami City Code Ch. 36 (see also Miami-Dade County Code § 21-28)

enforcement.howToReport: Call the police non-emergency line for an in-progress disturbance; chronic issues can be reported to City of Miami code enforcement.
